channel.title: Chloevolution
channel.description: Chloe D's technical blog covering Marketing, AI, SaaS, Fintech. Sharing insights with 7 years of experience in digital marketing.
channel.copyright: This work is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 International License.

item.title: The Complete Guide to Obsidian Plugins
item.author: Chloe
item.description:
When I first used Obsidian, I thought “it’s okay, just a note-taking app.”

The interface was clean, the features were adequate, but nothing special. After a few days, I was even a bit disappointed—is this the “most powerful note-taking tool” everyone talks about?

Until I started installing plugins.

After installing the Calendar plugin, my Daily Notes suddenly had a calendar view. After installing Templater, my templates became intelligent. After installing Dataview, my notes became a queryable database.

item.title: How to Use Obsidian Daily Notes: Complete Beginner's Guide
item.author: Chloe
item.description:
Opening a blank note, staring at the blinking cursor, not knowing what to write—does this sound familiar?

That’s exactly how I felt when I first started using Obsidian. Every day I’d think “I should write something down,” but when I actually opened a note, I had no idea where to start. Sometimes I didn’t know what to record, other times I felt like “this little thing isn’t worth creating a dedicated note for.” The result? Ideas would spin around in my head for a while, then disappear.

item.title: Complete Guide to Obsidian Templates: From Basics to Advanced, with 7 Practical Templates
item.author: Chloe
item.description:
When taking notes in Obsidian, have you noticed yourself repeating the same tasks? Every time you create a book note, you manually enter fields like “title, author, reading date”; every time you write a Daily Note, you recreate the same heading structure; every project note requires building the “goals, tasks, progress” framework from scratch.

These repetitive tasks not only waste time but also lead to inconsistent note formatting. When your vault contains hundreds of notes, format chaos makes information retrieval difficult.

item.title: Interactive Page SEO Strategy: Traffic Growth Secrets from Canva, Shopify, and NerdWallet
item.author: Chloe
item.description:
You’re designing your first poster and want to understand “what is a color wheel.” You search, click the first result—Canva’s color theory page.

On the right side of the page is a colorful circle. You casually click on orange, and the screen immediately shows that its complementary color is blue. Interesting. You try purple and see yellow. Then you start wondering: what does triadic color scheme look like? What about analogous colors?

item.title: Canva SEO Strategy Deep Dive: The Programmatic Growth Journey from 0 to 320 Million Monthly Visits
item.author: Chloe
item.description:
In 2012, three founders established Canva in Sydney, Australia, attempting to solve a simple problem: enabling people without design skills to create professional design work.

14 years later, Canva’s numbers are staggering (data source: SEMrush):

  • US Market Monthly Visits: 39.77 million
  • Keyword Coverage: 3.67 million
  • Indexed Pages: 190,000+
  • Company Valuation: $26 billion (2021)

In the US market, paid traffic accounts for only 0.1% of Canva’s total traffic. Their traffic growth is almost entirely driven by organic search.

item.title: How Do Claude Code, Gemini, and ChatGPT Find Questions Users Care About?
item.author: Chloe
item.description:
To enrich my existing AI-related articles, I wanted to supplement them with more user-interest-driven questions. So I asked Claude Code, Gemini, and ChatGPT the same question, hoping they could help identify relevant user queries.

During this process, I noticed significant differences in how each AI approached the task and the results they ultimately delivered. This article documents those observations and reflections.

What Question Did I Ask?

I sent the following prompt to each AI:

item.title: How to Use Skills in Claude Code?
item.author: Chloe
item.description:
Claude Code is an agentic command-line tool launched by Anthropic, and Skills are its core extension mechanism.

Simply put, Skills are a packaged set of instructions, scripts, and resources used to teach Claude how to complete specific tasks in a standardized way.

What are Claude Code Skills?

In Claude Code, Skills are like adding “professional plugins” or SOPs (Standard Operating Procedures) to AI.

They are Markdown-based instructions stored in specific folders, sometimes including executable scripts. Their core value is to eliminate the need to repeatedly input the same preferences, workflows, or domain knowledge in every conversation.

item.title: The Complete Guide to Meta Ads Library Search
item.author: Chloe
item.description:
In 2021, when I took over a client’s Meta advertising account for an e-commerce business, their ROAS was only 1.8. The budget was limited, the creative team consisted of just one person, and the competitors were brands backed by tens of millions in funding.

The first thing I did was not adjust bids or audiences. Instead, I spent three full days deconstructing 847 ads from 12 competitors in the Meta Ads Library. I recorded each ad’s runtime, creative format, copy structure, and CTA strategy, and even used Python scripts to batch-download all the assets.

item.title: From “Chatbox” to “Workflow”: Deconstructing Claude Code SEO Skills
item.author: Chloe
item.description:
Have you ever tried typing “please help me optimize this title” into the web versions of ChatGPT or Claude for SEO purposes? In that mode, AI is merely an “advanced copywriting assistant”—limited by outdated training data and completely unaware of your website’s real technical details.

With the emergence of Claude Code and its Skills system, AI is no longer just sitting on the other side of the screen chatting with you. It now effectively “lives” in your terminal, picks up crawling tools, and begins auditing your code like a seasoned SEO expert.

item.title: CLI vs GUI vs Skills
item.author: Chloe
item.description:
In 2026, mainstream AI productivity tools are undergoing a significant reconstruction of interaction logic. Claude Code by Anthropic and Google’s Gemini CLI mark a shift in core operating environments from traditional graphical user interfaces (GUI) back to command-line interfaces (CLI). The essence of this trend lies in improving the execution efficiency of complex tasks through a division of labor across different interaction layers.

Over the past decades, software development has primarily focused on lowering the barrier to entry through graphical interfaces. However, technological developments in 2026 show that top-tier AI tools are prioritizing the integration of command-line interfaces (CLI)—that is, controlling system-level operations through specific text-based instructions. This shift is not a regression in technology, but rather a result of AI’s ability to process text commands with significantly greater speed and accuracy than simulating actions within visual interfaces.
